title: Listening - Part 4: Social Media for Professionals
description: You will hear five short extracts in which people talk about using social media for their work. You must complete two tasks while listening.

content.audio_script: Speaker 1: As a freelance illustrator, [21]I mainly started a professional profile because I wanted to keep a clear boundary between my personal life and my work[/21]. I was tired of friends seeing my rough sketches. It’s been useful, but [26]the real benefit has been the speed at which I get feedback now[/26]. I can post a concept and know within an hour if it resonates with people. It’s like having a global focus group at your fingertips 24/7. || Speaker 2: I’m in real estate, and [22]I resisted social media until my boss made it mandatory for all senior agents[/22]. I thought it was a waste of time. I’ve been proven wrong. [27]I’ve found that it’s an incredible tool for keeping tabs on what the competition is up to[/27]. I can see their new listings and marketing strategies instantly. It keeps me on my toes and ensures I don't fall behind the market trends. || Speaker 3: I run a small organic bakery. [23]I went online because I was struggling to compete with the big supermarket chains[/23] and needed a way to reach locals directly. It’s been a lifesaver. What I value most, though, is [28]how it’s allowed me to build a brand personality that people actually trust[/28]. I post videos of the baking process, and customers feel like they know me. That loyalty is something money can’t buy. || Speaker 4: I’m a journalist, so for me, [24]social media was a way to find stories that weren't being covered by the mainstream press[/24]. It’s a goldmine for original leads. However, [29]I’ve been surprised by the level of technical support I can find there[/29]. Whenever I have a glitch with my editing software, there’s always a community of experts willing to talk me through a fix for free. It’s saved me hours of frustration. || Speaker 5: I’m a corporate lawyer, and [25]I use social media primarily to establish myself as a thought leader in my niche[/25]. I post articles on new regulations to show I’m an expert. It’s brought in some high-profile clients, but [30]what I really appreciate is how it simplifies the process of organising industry meet-ups[/30]. I can set up an event and invite five hundred specialists with one click. It’s made networking far more efficient than it used to be.

task1.title: Task One
task1.instruction: For questions 21 – 25, choose from the list (A – H) why each speaker started using social media for work.

questions.speaker: Speaker 1
questions.explanation: Speaker 1 wanted to 'keep a clear boundary' between personal and professional life.

questions.speaker: Speaker 2
questions.explanation: Speaker 2 started because their 'boss made it mandatory'.

questions.speaker: Speaker 3
questions.explanation: Speaker 3 needed to 'reach locals directly' to compete with supermarkets.

questions.speaker: Speaker 4
questions.explanation: Speaker 4 wanted to 'find stories' not covered by mainstream press.

questions.speaker: Speaker 5
questions.explanation: Speaker 5 uses it to 'establish myself as a thought leader' and expert.

options.A: to follow a trend in their industry
options.D: to separate their public and private personas
options.F: to comply with a management directive

task2.title: Task Two
task2.instruction: For questions 26 – 30, choose from the list (A – H) what each speaker currently finds most useful about it.

questions.speaker: Speaker 1
questions.explanation: Speaker 1 values the 'speed at which I get feedback' from their audience.

questions.speaker: Speaker 2
questions.explanation: Speaker 2 uses it for 'keeping tabs on what the competition is up to'.

questions.speaker: Speaker 3
questions.explanation: Speaker 3 values the 'brand personality' and trust they have built.

questions.speaker: Speaker 4
questions.explanation: Speaker 4 found 'technical support' and expert help for software issues.

questions.speaker: Speaker 5
questions.explanation: Speaker 5 likes how it 'simplifies the process of organising industry meet-ups'.

options.C: the immediate response from the public
options.D: the ease of tracking industry news
options.E: the insight into rival companies' activities
